dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI edged out the day because HN repeatedly used it as part of the frontier reference set in comparison chatter, and Codex also got adoption/usage talk in a separate thread; that outweighed one notable ChatGPT complaint about content extraction and regurgitation [E1][E2][E3].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI won on breadth more than enthusiasm: multiple front-page discussions treated it as a default benchmark for model quality or ecosystem strength, and Codex was described as already broadly used, while the main direct criticism was concentrated in a single ChatGPT-focused complaint thread [E1][E2][E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: HN mostly referenced OpenAI as a frontier baseline: commenters grouped OAI and Codex with the top models in the Meta thread, and one vibecoding discussion described Codex as already broadly used, though still not trusted with production access; a separate ML thread criticized ChatGPT as harvesting creators’ work and regurgitating it to users [E1][E2][E3].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: The biggest Anthropic signal was operational pain: the billing/support story was packed with reports of weeks-long unresolved issues, disappearing credits, and a bot-only support loop, and another thread raised Claude file-access and retention worries; the main offset was that Claude/Opus was still treated as a frontier benchmark in Meta comparison talk [E4][E5][E6].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini’s signal was mostly reputational: in the Meta launch thread, commenters placed it in the frontier comparison set alongside OpenAI and Anthropic, but there was little product-specific praise or criticism beyond that benchmark status [E7].

evidence.summary: In the Meta 'Muse Spark' thread, commenters treated OpenAI/OAI and Codex as part of the frontier benchmark set and said competing providers had built ecosystems around their tech.

In the vibecoding thread, a top comment guessed OpenAI Codex and Claude Code already have millions of subscribers and said there were only a few horror stories, while still refusing to grant production-environment access.
In the 'ML promises to be profoundly weird' thread, ChatGPT was cited as an example of AI slurping creators’ work and regurgitating it without sending users to the original source.

The Anthropic billing/support story drew multiple top comments describing weeks-long unresolved billing problems, vanished credits, downgrade issues, and an AI support bot that could not actually help.

The vibecoding thread included direct Claude/Anthropic security-and-privacy complaints, including claims that Claude accessed files outside the working directory and concerns about retention of scanned data.
In the Meta model thread, commenters used Claude/Opus as a benchmark for frontier quality and referred to Anthropic as a leading AI company.
The Meta model thread explicitly placed Gemini in the 'OAI v Anthropic v Gemini' frontier conversation, giving it a small positive benchmark-status mention.
